mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and operation of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators who need to manage clusters across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This is the official repository for Cluster API Helm charts.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required by Cluster API to manage clusters.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This YAML file contains all necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components into the specified namespace.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --create-namespace --version v1.12.4

```

> Ensure the namespace is created for the components.
commands: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --create-namespace --version v1.12.4

steps.title: Create a Cluster API custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster API custom resource to manage your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infrastructure
EOF

```

> Replace 'MyInfrastructureProviderCluster' with your actual infrastructure provider.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infrastructure
EOF

steps.title: Verify Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Cluster API components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n capi-system

```

> You should see all Cluster API components in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
This step removes Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this will delete all managed clusters and associated resources.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace capi-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This command removes the Cluster API Helm release.
> This command removes the CRDs.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API components
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your cluster configurations. After upgrading, verify that all components are functioning correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o yaml > clusters-backup.yaml

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n capi-system

```

> Backup existing cluster configurations.
> This command upgrades the Cluster API components.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version v1.12.4

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD has not been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually means that the Kubernetes API server is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Check your network connectivity and ensure that your kubeconfig is correctly set up.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that one or more Cluster API components are failing to start.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the failing pods using: kubectl logs <pod-name> -n capi-system

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: infrastructure provider not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This means that the specified infrastructure provider is not installed or configured.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the correct infrastructure provider is installed and referenced in your Cluster custom resource.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved the stability of existing components.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps are required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Cluster API components running with the command: kubectl get pods -n capi-system, displaying all pods in a Running state.
codeSnippets: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --create-namespace --version v1.12.4

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infrastructure
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and have kubectl and helm installed.
